summary: This is an extremely brief post consisting of a single rhetorical survey question directed at readers, implying that Netscape 6 shipped with obvious, critical bugs. Joel uses the format of a reader poll to make a pointed critique of Netscape 6's quality at launch, suggesting that show-stopper bugs were so prevalent they could be found almost immediately.
key_points: Netscape 6 launched with easily discoverable critical bugs
The rhetorical framing ('how long') implies the bugs were found nearly instantly by anyone who used it
Joel uses humor and audience participation to make his quality critique more devastating than a straightforward review
money_quotes: How long did it take you to find the first 3 show-stopper bugs in Netscape 6?
key_insight: When show-stopper bugs are so obvious that finding three is a matter of minutes, the product should not have shipped.
